channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I researched 100 ink cartridges and, at almost $10,000 per litre, this very popular HP cartridge is the most expensive in the world
item.description: When choosing an ink cartridge for your printer, look beyond the shelf price, It is important to consider the total number of pages that can be printed.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Donald Trump is losing on TikTok
item.description: TikTok’s influence has become undeniable in the 2024 presidential election, with both camps leaning full force into the idea that if you can’t beat ’em, join ’em. But that plan hasn’t worked out so well for Republican nominee Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Massachusetts judge rules Meta must face state's social media lawsuit
item.description: A Massachusetts Superior Court judge has ruled Meta must face a state lawsuit, alleging the company knowingly designed and deployed features harmful to young online users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Samsung’s $17 billion Texas chip factory faces another setback. Here’s why
item.description: Samsung Electronics has postponed taking deliveries of ASML chipmaking equipment for its upcoming factory in Texas as it has yet to win any major customers for the project, three people familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: The U.S. government’s road safety agency is investigating Tesla’s “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This gigayacht has a glass-bottom pool and stunning underwater lounge
item.description: An architecture firm has unveiled the concept for a 454-foot gigayacht that combines luxury, innovation and style and can accommodate 22 guests and 52 crew members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta partners with horror movie maker Blumhouse Productions to test Movie Gen
item.description: Facebook owner Meta said on Thursday it had teamed up with Blumhouse Productions, the Hollywood company behind popular horror films such as The Purge and Get Out, to put its new generative AI video model Movie Gen to the test.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei pens a smart look at our AI future
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: One Direction was the internet's first boy band, and Liam Payne its grounding force
item.description: One Direction member Liam Payne was found dead this week after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, Argentina, at just 31.
item.dc:creator: Maria Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: An Alabama man has been arrested for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok let through disinformation in political ads despite its own ban, Global Witness finds
item.description: Just weeks before the U.S. presidential election, TikTok still approved advertisements that contained election disinformation even though it has a ban on political ads.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SpaceX sues California regulator, claiming its launches were blocked because of Elon Musk’s politics
item.description: SpaceX claimed in a lawsuit filed Tuesday that members of the California Coastal Commission violated Musk’s right to free speech and overstepped the agency’s authority after members cited the billionaire’s conspiratorial comments.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Chinese cybersecurity association urges review of Intel products
item.description: The Cybersecurity Association of China (CSAC) has urged a security review of Intel products sold in the country, claiming the US semiconductor firm poses ongoing threats to China’s national security and interests.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Secure Azure Kubernetes with Advanced Container Networking Services
item.description: Microsoft’s Azure Kubernetes Service is designed to take care of much of the day-to-day management of Kubernetes, simplifying a lot of the complexity that goes with building and running large-scale, cloud-native applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Big Tech turns to nuclear energy to power AI boom
item.description: Major technology companies are increasingly turning to nuclear energy as they attempt to keep up with the vast energy demands of artificial intelligence (AI) without derailing their highly publicized environmental goals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's Robovan, Robotaxi, humanoid robots
item.description: Tesla's "We, Robot" event reveals the Robovan, Robotaxi and the Optimus humanoid robot. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the company's latest innovations in autonomous technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Hacked robo-vacuums chase owners, yell slurs
item.description: Some customers report the tiny tech tools chased them around their homes and barraged them with profanities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach
item.description: A new national strategy warns that counterintelligence pros have to work more closely with other agencies and the private sector to address threats. The post NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How to choose a flexible unified SASE solution
item.description: SASE is at the top of all CIOs’ minds, and for good reason. When deployed properly, SASE, which combines SD-WAN with cloud-delivered security via SSE, enables all users and devices, regardless of location, to securely access an organization’s hybrid network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hide and lock your apps on your iPhone with iOS 18
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the iOS 18’s new features to hide and lock apps for better privacy and organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to leverage APIs for IT-enabled information capability
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses face constant pressure to adapt or risk being left behind. The strategic use of IT-enabled information management capability (IMC) is no longer just an advantage — it’s a necessity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Musk's Starlink Internet free for hurricane victims through 2024
item.description: Following criticism, the company issued a new offer that extends the free service through the end of the year for victims of both hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation
